The naturallinewidth of a quantum state is set by the time–energy uncertainty principle, ΔE ≈ ħ/τ.  

- For τ₁ = 10⁻⁹ s, ΔE₁ ≈ ħ/10⁻⁹ ≈ 6.6 × 10⁻⁷ eV.  
- For τ₂ = 10⁻⁸ s, ΔE₂ ≈ ħ/10⁻⁸ ≈ 6.6 × 10⁻⁸ eV.

The existence of isolated magnetic poles (magnetic monopoles) would modify Maxwell’s equations in a symmetric way: the divergence of the magnetic field would no longer be zero, and the curl of the electric field would acquire an extra term involving magnetic current. \]
